title: The Nature of the Self
summary: Dattatreya describes the all-pervading Self which is free from division, space, time, and modifications. Addresses feeling disconnected, fear of isolation, and existential anxiety.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “The desire to gain the knowledge of non-duality arises in humans by the grace of the universal mind, which relieves them of greatest fears.” and develops toward “Like all those scriptures say, Consciousness is very pure, devoid of any qualities or imperfections. It has no body or form, it is the only Truth.…”

This passage begins with “Know that everything that has a form is virtual, an illusion. The formless one is eternal and true. Once you understand this teaching, reincarnation will be impossible.” and develops toward “I am not at all that which acts, eats, performs rituals or donates etc. I am pure, unborn, immortal Self.”

This passage begins with “Know that the whole existence is formless or empty, it is changeless, pure, even though seems to have a form and its nature is Consciousness.” and develops toward “If I am omnipresent, effulgent, changeless, perfect and eternal, how can I see the difference between internal or external?”

This passage begins with “The existence is bright, undivided and continuous. Maya, attachments, duality, non-duality are all imaginations.” and develops toward “There exists no nothingness or something-ness, there are no truths or untruths. One speaks the scriptures out of one’s own nature.”

verses.sanskrit: ईश्वरानुग्रहादेव पुंसामद्वैतवासना । महद्भयपरित्राणाद्विप्राणामुपजायते ।।१।।
verses.translation: The desire to gain the knowledge of non-duality arises in humans by the grace of the universal mind, which relieves them of greatest fears.

verses.sanskrit: येनेदं पूरितं सर्वमात्मनैवाअत्मनात्मनि । निराकारं कथं वन्दे ह्यभिन्नं शिवमव्ययम् ।।२।।
verses.translation: How can I greet that Consciousness which is formless, complete, undivided, pervades everything and all beings?

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चभूतात्मकं विश्वं मरीचिजलसन्निभम् । कस्याप्यहो नमस्कुर्यामहमेको निरञ्जनः ।। ३।।
verses.translation: The physical world is a mirage. I alone exist as most pure. Whom should I bow to?

verses.sanskrit: आत्मैव केवलं सर्वं भेदाभेदो न विद्यते । अस्ति नास्ति कथं ब्रूयां विस्मयः प्रतिभाति मे ।। ४।।
verses.translation: Consciousness is everything, differences or similarities in it cannot be known. I am filled with wonder, how to say that it exists or does not exist?

verses.sanskrit: वेदान्तसारसर्वस्वं ज्ञानं विज्ञानमेव च । अहमात्मा निराकारः सर्वव्यापी स्वभावतः ।। ५।।
verses.translation: The essence of Vedanta philosophy, all knowledge and sciences is that the nature of the Consciousness is omnipresence and formlessness.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै सर्वात्मको देवो निष्कलो गगनोपमः । स्वभावनिर्मलः शुद्धः स एवायं न संशयः ।। ६।।
verses.translation: There is no doubt that the universal Consciousness is by nature very pure, taintless, effulgent, and one, just like clear sky.

verses.sanskrit: अहमेवाव्ययोऽनन्तः शुद्धविज्ञानविग्रहः । सुखं दुःखं न जानामि कथं कस्यापि वर्तते ।। ७।।
verses.translation: I, Consciousness, am pure, indestructible, infinite, I am pure knowledge itself. Do not know joy or sorrow, what to say about them?

verses.sanskrit: न मानसं कर्म शुभाशुभं मे न कायिकं कर्म शुभाशुभं मे । न वाचिकं कर्म शुभाशुभं मे ज्ञानामृतं शुद्धमतीन्द्रियोऽहम् ।।८।।
verses.translation: I have no mental actions, neither good nor bad. No bodily and verbal actions too, neither good nor bad. I am the essence of knowledge and am beyond the senses. I am so pure.

verses.sanskrit: मनो वै गगनाकारं मनो वै सर्वतोमुखम् । मनोऽतीतं मनः सर्वं न मनः परमार्थतः ।।९।।
verses.translation: Mind is vast. Mind is multifaceted. Mind is time or past memories. Everything that is manifested is Mind. However, if seen from highest perspective, there is no Mind.

verses.sanskrit: अहमेकमिदं सर्वं व्योमातीतं निरन्तरम् । पश्यामि कथमात्मानं प्रत्यक्षं वा तिरोहितम् ।। १०।।
verses.translation: I am the only one that exists. I am beyond space, unbroken and continuous. How can the Consciousness be perceived directly or indirectly?

verses.sanskrit: त्वमेवमेकं हि कथं न बुध्यसे समं हि सर्वेषु विमृष्टमव्ययम् । सदोदितोऽसि त्वमखण्डितः प्रभो दिवा च नक्तं च कथं हि मन्यसे ।। ११।।
verses.translation: Why don’t you understand that you, the Consciousness, are one. It is the same one in everyone. It is ever present, ever shining. How can you say it varies like day and night?

verses.sanskrit: आत्मानं सततं विद्धि सर्वत्रैकं निरन्तरम् । अहं ध्याता परं ध्येयमखण्डं खण्ड्यते कथम् ।। १२।।
verses.translation: Know Consciousness as continuous, one everywhere, unbroken and changeless. How can you possibly divide yourself, the indivisible Consciousness into a meditator and an object of meditation?

verses.sanskrit: न जातो न मृतोऽसि त्वं न ते देहः कदाचन । सर्वं ब्रह्मेति विख्यातं ब्रवीति बहुधा श्रुतिः ।। १३।।
verses.translation: You were never a body, you do not die nor were born. The famous scriptures often say – Everything is “All that is”.

verses.sanskrit: स बाह्याभ्यन्तरोऽसि त्वं शिवः सर्वत्र सर्वदा । इतस्ततः कथं भ्रान्तः प्रधावसि पिशाचवत् ।। १४।।
verses.translation: Internally or externally, you are just one Consciousness, everywhere and all the time. Why are you running around in delusion here and there like a ghost?

verses.sanskrit: संयोगश्च वियोगश्च वर्तते न च ते न मे । न त्वं नाहं जगन्नेदं सर्वमात्मैव केवलम् ।। १५।।
verses.translation: Union or divisions do not exist in me or you. Everything is Consciousness alone, there is no separate you, me or the world.

verses.sanskrit: शब्दादिपञ्चकस्यास्य लैवासि त्वं न ते पुनः। त्वमेव परमं तत्त्वमतः किं परितप्यसे ।। १५।।
verses.translation: You are not the object of five senses, such as sounds etc. You, the Consciousness, is the primordial substance, the only reality. Why do you suffer at all?

verses.sanskrit: जन्म मृत्युर्न ते चित्तं बन्धमोक्षौ शुभाशुभौ । कथं रोदिषि रे वत्स नामरूपं न ते न मे ।। १७।।
verses.translation: There is no birth or death for you, and you are not bounded or liberated. You are not a mind. You are not good or evil. Why do you cry, my son? Both you and I are not forms or names.

verses.sanskrit: अहो चित्त कथं भ्रान्तः प्रधावसि पिशाचवत् । अभिन्नं पश्य चात्मानं रागत्यागात्सुखी भव ।। १८।।
verses.translation: Mind, why are you running around in delusion, like a ghost? See that you are not different from the Consciousness, give up attachments and be happy.

verses.sanskrit: त्वमेव तत्त्वं हि विकारवर्जितं निष्कम्पमेकं हि विमोक्षविग्रहम् । न ते च रागो ह्यथवा विरागः कथं हि सन्तप्यसि कामकामतः ।। १९।।
verses.translation: Your reality or substance is not modifiable. It cannot be shaken, joined or disintegrated. You never had any attachments or aversions. Why are you suffering because of all these random desires?

verses.sanskrit: वदन्ति श्रुतयः सर्वाः निर्गुणं शुद्धमव्ययम् । अशरीरं समं तत्त्वं तन्मां विद्धि न संशयः ।। २०।।
verses.translation: Like all those scriptures say, Consciousness is very pure, devoid of any qualities or imperfections. It has no body or form, it is the only Truth. There is no doubt that I am that.

verses.sanskrit: साकारमनृतं विद्धि निराकारं निरन्तरम् । एतत्तत्त्वोपदेशेन न पुनर्भवसम्भवः ।। २१।।
verses.translation: Know that everything that has a form is virtual, an illusion. The formless one is eternal and true. Once you understand this teaching, reincarnation will be impossible.

verses.sanskrit: एकमेव समं तत्त्वं वदन्ति हि विपश्चितः । रागत्यागात्पुनश्चित्तमेकानेकं न विद्यते ।। २२।।
verses.translation: Seers say that truth is oneness. Mind, when detached, does not see the divisions in oneness anymore.

verses.sanskrit: अनात्मरूपं च कथं समाधि- रात्मस्वरूपं च कथं समाधिः । अस्तीति नास्तीति कथं समाधि- र्मोक्षस्वरूपं यदि सर्वमेकम् ।। २३।।
verses.translation: How can something that is not Consciousness be in Samadhi? How can something that is already Consciousness be in Samadhi? How can something that is ever one and has ultimate freedom, exists as well as not exist, be in Samadhi?

verses.sanskrit: विशुद्धोऽसि समं तत्त्वं विदेहस्त्वमजोऽव्ययः । जानामीह न जानामीत्यात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। २४।।
verses.translation: I am disembodied, formless, changeless pure Truth. How can you say I know the Consciousness or I don’t know it?

verses.sanskrit: तत्त्वमस्यादिवाक्येन स्वात्मा हि प्रतिपादितः । नेति नेति श्रुतिर्ब्रूयादनृतं पाञ्चभौतिकम् ।। २५।।
verses.translation: As the scriptures say, “I am that” and such sentences establish the truth of the Consciousness. As they say – it is not composed of the elements, it is “not this” “not that”.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मन्येवात्मना सर्वं त्वया पूर्णं निरन्तरम् । ध्याता ध्यानं न ते चित्तं निर्लज्जं ध्यायते कथम् ।। २६।।
verses.translation: You are Consciousness and everything is Consciousness, which is complete and unbroken without boundaries. You are not the meditator nor the object of meditation. Why does this shameless mind keep meditating?

verses.sanskrit: शिवं न जानामि कथं वदामि शिवं न जानामि कथं भजामि । अहं शिवश्चेत्परमार्थथतत्त्वं समस्वरूपं गगनोपमं च ।। २७।।
verses.translation: I do not know what is Consciousness, how can I even talk about it or worship it? I am Consciousness, like sky or empty space. That is the ultimate Truth.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं तत्त्वं समं तत्त्वं कल्पनाहेतुवर्जितम् । ग्राह्यग्राहकनिर्मुक्तं स्वसंवेद्यं कथं भवेत् ।। २८।।
verses.translation: I am not like a truth that can be imagined. How can I know myself via a relation like giver and given?

verses.sanskrit: अनन्तरूपं न हि वस्तु किंचि- त्तत्त्वस्वरूपं न हि वस्तु किंचित् । आत्मैकरूपं परमार्थतत्त्वं न हिंसको वापि न चाप्यहिंसा ।। २९।।
verses.translation: No object is infinite, no object has any reality. Only Consciousness is the ultimate Truth. It cannot be destroyed nor can it destroy.

verses.sanskrit: विशुद्धोऽसि समं तत्त्वं विदेहमजमव्ययम् । विभ्रमं कथमात्मार्थे विभ्रान्तोऽहं कथं पुनः ।। ३०।।
verses.translation: I am pure, formless, unborn, imperishable and the only Truth. How can there be any confusion about this Consciousness? How can I get confused ever again after knowing this?

verses.sanskrit: घटे भिन्ने घटाकाशं सुलीनं भेदवर्जितम् । शिवेन मनसा शुद्धो न भेदः प्रतिभाति मे ।। ३१।।
verses.translation: Once a clay pot is broken, the space inside it cannot be differentiated from space surrounding it. Once the mind is purified of ignorance, I cannot see any difference between the Mind and the Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: न घटो न घटाकाशो न जीवो न जीवविग्रहः । केवलं ब्रह्म संविद्धि वेद्यवेदकवर्जितम् ।। ३२।।
verses.translation: Ultimately, there is no clay pot nor is there any space in the pot. Similarly, there are no disembodied or embodied minds. Know that there is just “All that is”, with no division of the experience and the experiencer.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वत्र सर्वदा सर्वमात्मानं सततं ध्रुवम् । सर्वं शून्यमशून्यं च तन्मां विद्धि न संशयः ।। ३३।।
verses.translation: Consciousness is everything, it is everywhere eternally present. It is something and nothing at once, know this, have no doubts about it.

verses.sanskrit: वेदा न लोका न सुरा न यज्ञा वर्णाश्रमो नैव कुलं न जातिः । न धूममार्गो न च दीप्तिमार्गो ब्रह्मैकरूपं परमार्थतत्त्वम् ।। ३४।।
verses.translation: There are no scriptures or Vedas, no worlds, no gods, no offering ceremonies. There are no races, no lineages, no castes. There are no paths of darkness or of light. The ultimate Truth is “All that is”, oneness without form.

verses.sanskrit: व्याप्यव्यापकनिर्मुक्तः त्वमेकः सफलं यदि । प्रत्यक्षं चापरोक्षं च ह्यात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। ३५।।
verses.translation: If you are the one, devoid of containers and contents, how can you think that the Consciousness is directly witnessed?

verses.sanskrit: अद्वैतं केचिदिच्छन्ति द्वैतमिच्छन्ति चापरे । समं तत्त्वं न विन्दन्ति द्वैताद्वैतविवर्जितम् ।। ३६।।
verses.translation: Some favour non-dualism, others favour dualism. They do not know that the ultimate Truth is beyond dualism or non-dualism.

verses.sanskrit: श्वेतादिवर्णरहितं शब्दादिगुणवर्जितम् । कथयन्ति कथं तत्त्वं मनोवाचामगोचरम् ।। ३७।।
verses.translation: How can they even talk about this truth which is beyond the reach of the mind and language, which is not white or any other colour, which is devoid of sounds or any other qualia?

verses.sanskrit: यदाऽनृतमिदं सर्वं देहादिगगनोपमम् । तदा हि ब्रह्म संवेत्ति न ते द्वैतपरम्परा ।। ३८।।
verses.translation: When everything start appearing false, illusory, including the body, like empty space, then you know the Brahman, not through any dualistic traditions.

verses.sanskrit: परेण सहजात्मापि ह्यभिन्नः प्रतिभाति मे । व्योमाकारं तथैवैकं ध्याता ध्यानं कथं भवेत् ।। ३९।।
verses.translation: I see others also as myself naturally, not different, as just this Consciousness. How can the one which is like empty space become meditator and meditation?

verses.sanskrit: यत्करोमि यदश्नामि यज्जुहोमि ददामि यत् । एतत्सर्वं न मे किंचिद्विशुद्धोऽहमजोऽव्ययः ।। ४०।।
verses.translation: I am not at all that which acts, eats, performs rituals or donates etc. I am pure, unborn, immortal Self.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं जगद्विद्धि निराकृतीदं सर्वं जगद्विद्धि विकारहीनम् । सर्वं जगद्विद्धि विशुद्धदेह सर्वं जगद्विद्धि शिवैकरूपम् ।। ४१।।
verses.translation: Know that the whole existence is formless or empty, it is changeless, pure, even though seems to have a form and its nature is Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: तत्त्वं त्वं न हि सन्देहः किं जानाम्यथवा पुनः । असंवेद्यं स्वसंवेद्यमात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। ४२।।
verses.translation: There is no doubt that you the Consciousness is the Truth. What else can you know apart from this? How can you call that which knows itself as the unknown?

verses.sanskrit: मायाऽमाया कथं तात छायाऽछाया न विद्यते । तत्त्वमेकमिदं सर्वं व्योमाकारं निरञ्जनम् ।। ४३।।
verses.translation: My son, I do not see how there can be separation of one into illusion and reality, or shadow and light. It is all one Truth, untainted, undifferentiated like empty space.

verses.sanskrit: आदिमध्यान्तमुक्तोऽहं न बद्धोऽहं कदाचन । स्वभावनिर्मलः शुद्ध इति मे निश्चिता मतिः ।। ४४।।
verses.translation: I was free in the beginning, free now and will be free till the end, I was never bound. I am naturally very clean and pure, this is my firm understanding.

verses.sanskrit: महदादि जगत्सर्वं न किंचित्प्रतिभाति मे । ब्रह्मैव केवलं सर्वं कथं वर्णाश्रमस्थितिः ।। ४५।।
verses.translation: I do not ever see this whole universe, not even the first substance. Oneness is All that is. How can it be differentiated into races or castes?

verses.sanskrit: जानामि सर्वथा सर्वमहमेको निरन्तरम् । निरालम्बमशून्यं च शून्यं व्योमादिपञ्चकम् ।। ४६।।
verses.translation: I know that I am the only one there always, seen as everything. Space and physical elements are empty and have no foundation of their own.

verses.sanskrit: न षण्ढो न पुमान्न स्त्री न बोधो नैव कल्पना । सानन्दो वा निरानन्दमात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। ४७।।
verses.translation: Consciousness has no gender, it is not a man, woman or an eunuch. It is not an imagination or an idea in the mind. How can you believe that it has any happiness or suffering?

verses.sanskrit: षडङ्गयोगान्न तु नैव शुद्धं मनोविनाशान्न तु नैव शुद्धम् । गुरूपदेशान्न तु नैव शुद्धं स्वयं च तत्त्वं स्वयमेव बुद्धम् ।। ४८।।
verses.translation: Consciousness cannot be purified by the six limbed yoga practices. Cannot be purified by stopping or destroying one’s mind. Cannot be purified by teachings of a Guru. It is itself the Truth, it knows itself as beyond mind.

verses.sanskrit: न हि पञ्चात्मको देहो विदेहो वर्तते न हि । आत्मैव केवलं सर्वं तुरीयं च त्रयं कथम् ।। ४९।।
verses.translation: Minds embodied in physical matter do not exist, nor are there any disembodied minds. When there is only Consciousness as everything, how can you talk of three or four states of minds?

verses.sanskrit: न बद्धो नैव मुक्तोऽहं न चाहं ब्रह्मणः पृथक् । न कर्ता न च भोक्ताहं व्याप्यव्यापकवर्जितः ।। ५०।।
verses.translation: I was never bound so I cannot be liberated. I was never differentiated from the oneness. I’m not the doer of actions, I am not the enjoyer of consequences, I am devoid of anything, just empty.

verses.sanskrit: यथा जलं जले न्यस्तं सलिलं भेदवर्जितम् । प्रकृतिं पुरुषं तद्वदभिन्नं प्रतिभाति मे ।। ५१।।
verses.translation: Just like the water when added to water cannot be differentiated, I cannot see any difference in experiences and the experiencer.

verses.sanskrit: यदि नाम न मुक्तोऽसि न बद्धोऽसि कदाचन । साकारं च निराकारमात्मानं मन्यसे कथम् ।। ५२।।
verses.translation: If it was never bound or liberated, how can the Consciousness be called as having a form or formless?

verses.sanskrit: जानामि ते परं रूपं प्रत्यक्षं गगनोपमम् । यथा परं हि रूपं यन्मरीचिजलसन्निभम् ।। ५३।।
verses.translation: Know this directly that your nature is like the metaphorical empty space. Rest is just like the illusory water that appears in a mirage.

verses.sanskrit: न गुरुर्नोपदेशश्च न चोपाधिर्न मे क्रिया । विदेहं गगनं विद्धि विशुद्धोऽहं स्वभावतः ।। ५४।।
verses.translation: I know that I am without form, pure, like limitless space naturally, not realized via a Guru’s teachings, or not because of any lofty titles or not through yogic kriyas.

verses.sanskrit: विशुद्धोऽस्य शरीरोऽसि न ते चित्तं परात्परम् । अहं चात्मा परं तत्त्वमिति वक्तुं न लज्जसे ।। ५५।।
verses.translation: Your highest essence is not the body or the mind, you are pure. You should not hesitate to say that I am the ultimate Truth, the Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: कथं रोदिषि रे चित्त ह्यात्मैवात्मात्मना भव । पिब वत्स कलातीतमद्वैतं परमामृतम् ।। ५६।।
verses.translation: Mind, why are you suffering? Be the Self, the Consciousness. My son, drink the eternal nectar of non-duality.

verses.sanskrit: नैव बोधो न चाबोधो न बोधाबोध एव च । यस्येदृशः सदा बोधः स बोधो नान्यथा भवेत् ।। ५७।।
verses.translation: There exists neither knowledge nor ignorance. That which has such knowledge is the knowledge itself, never otherwise.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानं न तर्को न समाधियोगो न देशकालौ न गुरूपदेशः । स्वभावसंवित्तरहं च तत्त्व- माकाशकल्पं सहजं ध्रुवं च ।। ५८।।
verses.translation: Knowledge or logic, samadhi or yoga, space or time, master’s teachings, all these are not needed to be that Truth which is like sky, naturally steadily present.

verses.sanskrit: न जातोऽहं मृतो वापि न मे कर्म शुभाशुभम् । विशुद्धं निर्गुणं ब्रह्म बन्धो मुक्तिः कथं मम ।। ५९।।
verses.translation: I do not take births, I do not die. I do no auspicious or inauspicious acts. How can there be bondage or liberation for that which is purest and is devoid of any qualities?

verses.sanskrit: यदि सर्वगतो देवः स्थिरः पूर्णो निरन्तरः । अन्तरं हि न पश्यामि स बाह्याभ्यन्तरः कथम् ।। ६०।।
verses.translation: If I am omnipresent, effulgent, changeless, perfect and eternal, how can I see the difference between internal or external?

verses.sanskrit: स्फुरत्येव जगत्कृत्स्नमखण्डितनिरन्तरम् । अहो मायामहामोहो द्वैताद्वैतविकल्पना ।। ६१।।
verses.translation: The existence is bright, undivided and continuous. Maya, attachments, duality, non-duality are all imaginations.

verses.sanskrit: साकारं च निराकारं नेति नेतीति सर्वदा । भेदाभेदविनिर्मुक्तो वर्तते केवलः शिवः ।। ६२।।
verses.translation: Everything that has form is not-this, everything that is formless is not-this. Consciousness exists free from any divisions or similarities.

verses.sanskrit: न ते च माता च पिता च बन्धुः न ते च पत्नी न सुतश्च मित्रम् । न पक्षपाती न विपक्षपातः कथं हि संतप्तिरियं हि चित्ते ।। ६३।।
verses.translation: You have no mother, father, brother, wife, son or friends. You do not favour anything or reject anything. How is this suffering still present in your mind?

verses.sanskrit: दिवा नक्तं न ते चित्तं उदयास्तमयौ न हि । विदेहस्य शरीरत्वं कल्पयन्ति कथं बुधाः ।। ६४।।
verses.translation: Your mind does not rise or set like the sun in a day. How can an intelligent man imagine the formless being taking up bodies?

verses.sanskrit: नाविभक्तं विभक्तं च न हि दुःखसुखादि च । न हि सर्वमसर्वं च विद्धि चात्मानमव्ययम् ।। ६५।।
verses.translation: Know the Consciousness as having no parts, its not all things or some things, it is not undivided or divided, and it has no happiness or suffering.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं कर्ता न भोक्ता च न मे कर्म पुराऽधुना । न मे देहो विदेहो वा निर्ममेति ममेति किम् ।। ६६।।
verses.translation: I am not the doer or enjoyer, do not have any old or new karma. I do not have a body nor am I bodiless. How can it be that “this is me” or “this is not me?

verses.sanskrit: न मे रागादिको दोषो दुःखं देहादिकं न मे । आत्मानं विद्धि मामेकं विशालं गगनोपमम् ।। ६७।।
verses.translation: I do not have any likes or dislikes, no imperfections, no sufferings or pains of the body. I know myself as one immensity, like that of the sky.

verses.sanskrit: सखे मनः किं बहुजल्पितेन सखे मनः सर्वमिदं वितर्क्यम् । यत्सारभूतं कथितं मया ते त्वमेव तत्त्वं गगनोपमोऽसि ।। ६८।।
verses.translation: O Mind, my friend, all this is of no consequence. O mind, my friend, all this is just arguments. I told you the essence of it all. You are the Truth, like the immense space.

verses.sanskrit: येन केनापि भावेन यत्र कुत्र मृता अपि । योगिनस्तत्र लीयन्ते घटाकाशमिवाम्बरे ।। ६९।।
verses.translation: However a yogi lives, where ever he dies, he is dissolved into everything like the space in a clay pot merges back into all space after it is broken.

verses.sanskrit: तीर्थे चान्त्यजगेहे वा नष्टस्मृतिरपि त्यजन् । समकाले तनुं मुक्तः कैवल्यव्यापको भवेत् ।। ७०।।
verses.translation: Does not matter if one leaves this world at a holy place or if one loses all memories also. He is freed immediately and merges into the ultimate existence.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मार्थकाममोक्षांश्च द्विपदादिचराचरम् । मन्यन्ते योगिनः सर्वं मरीचिजलसन्निभम् ।। ७१।।
verses.translation: A yogi sees the right conduct, prosperity, desires, liberation, men and all animated or unanimated, as merely like an illusion of water in a mirage.

verses.sanskrit: अतीतानागतं कर्म वर्तमानं तथैव च । न करोमि न भुञ्जामि इति मे निश्चला मतिः ।। ७२।।
verses.translation: I know certainly that I do not perform any actions, nor am I affected by their consequences, whether they are in past, present or future actions.

verses.sanskrit: शून्यागारे समरसपूत- स्तिष्ठन्नेकः सुखमवधूतः । चरति हि नग्नस्त्यक्त्वा गर्वं विन्दति केवलमात्मनि सर्वम् ।। ७३।।
verses.translation: With an empty mind like an unoccupied house, equanimous, an Avdhuta remains stable, renouncing pride, he wanders naked and knows everything as Consciousness itself.

verses.sanskrit: त्रितयतुरीयं नहि नहि यत्र विन्दति केवलमात्मनि तत्र । धर्माधर्मौ नहि नहि यत्र बद्धो मुक्तः कथमिह तत्र ।। ७४।।
verses.translation: There are no three or four states, I know there is only Consciousness. When there is no right or wrong conduct, how can there be bondage or liberation.

verses.sanskrit: विन्दति विन्दति नहि नहि मन्त्रं छन्दोलक्षणं नहि नहि तन्त्रम् । समरसमग्नो भावितपूतः प्रलपितमेतत्परमवधूतः ।। ७५।।
verses.translation: An Avdhuta knows no formulas, no verses, no techniques. He is unmoving, equanimous, and absorbed in the ultimate.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वशून्यमशून्यं च सत्यासत्यं न विद्यते । स्वभावभावतः प्रोक्तं शास्त्रसंवित्तिपूर्वकम् ।। ७६।।
verses.translation: There exists no nothingness or something-ness, there are no truths or untruths. One speaks the scriptures out of one’s own nature.
